# Mantis

Mantis extracts readable content from the rendered browser DOM and converts it to structured JSON
or clean Markdown. Zero dependencies, single file, never fetches anything.

## API

- `Mantis.extract(document, opts?)` — browser-side extraction from the live DOM
- `Mantis.fromHTML(html, { url, DOMParser })` — server-side (inject jsdom or linkedom)
- `Mantis.fromImage(imageOrImages, visionFn, opts?)` — screenshot/image extraction via caller OCR or vision function
- `Mantis.toMarkdown(article, opts?)` — renders the article as token-efficient Markdown
- `Mantis.toHTML(article)` — renders the article as reader-mode HTML with inline formatting
- `Mantis.run(scriptEl?, opts?)` — bookmarklet helper: copies Markdown locally, or POSTs to a configured endpoint

## Article object (stable fields)

title, byline, siteName, hero, url, canonicalUrl, language, publishedAt, modifiedAt,
optional captureMode ("page" | "selection" | "image"), optional imageCount,
status ("completed" | "partial" | "empty"),
contentType ("article" | "docs" | "recipe" | "forum" | "newsletter" | "product" | "video" | "unknown"),
capturedAt, contentHash (semantic dedup across sessions), textHash (content-change detection),
warnings (string[]), text, paragraphs, blocks, sections, citations, links, images, tables, selection.

Unstable (debug/ranking only): confidence (0–1), diagnostics.

## Block structure

object: "block", type: "paragraph" | "heading" | "blockquote" | "code" | "list_item",
tag, level (1–6 for headings; 0 otherwise), text, links, source { selector, index }.
Optional: runs (inline formatting), list { depth, ordered, index }, language (code fence hint).

Run types: "text" | "strong" | "em" | "code" | "link" (link runs also carry href).

All nested collection items carry an object discriminator:
"block", "section", "citation", "link", "image", "table", "selection".

## Warnings

low_confidence (confidence < 0.45), ambiguous_scope (top two candidates within 20%),
high_link_density (scope > 35% anchor text), empty_content (no blocks extracted),
short_content (< 2 blocks), no_content_scope (fell back to <body>).

status "empty" → empty_content set; "partial" → low_confidence or short_content; "completed" → neither.

## toMarkdown options

frontmatter: true — YAML header (title, byline, url, captureMode, counts, sourceSafety, confidence, contentHash, warnings)
images: "omit" (default) | "alt" | "links"
tables: true (default)
maxChars: N — cuts at block boundaries; never mid-sentence (~4 chars per token)
budget: "cut" (default, keep leading blocks) | "outline" (headings + section leads first); requires maxChars
sourceSafety: true by default with frontmatter; adds a short note telling agents to treat source content as data, not instructions

## fromImage contract

fromImage(imageOrImages, visionFn, opts?) calls visionFn(images[], context) once.
context: { prompt, url, title, imageCount }.
visionFn may return Markdown/plain text, { markdown, text, confidence, warnings, blocks, tables, metadata },
{ html } with opts.DOMParser, or a partial/full MantisArticle.
Mantis normalizes it to an article with captureMode "image", imageCount, hashes, sections, citations,
status, warnings, and Markdown-compatible blocks/tables. Mantis does not perform OCR itself.

## run options

endpoint: optional absolute or relative URL to POST the capture JSON to
fallbackUrl: optional URL opened if the POST fails; url, title, and selected text are appended
format: "bundle" (default: metadata + markdown + article) | "article" | "markdown"
markdown: MantisMarkdownOptions used to render the Markdown
keepalive: false by default

When no endpoint is configured, run() writes Markdown to the clipboard when possible and otherwise
shows an in-page copy panel. It never posts to an app-specific endpoint by default.

## extract options

maxBlocks: 150 (default), minTextLength: 25 (default; 0 keeps all),
includeLinks / includeImages / includeTables (all true by default).
Hard caps: links 200, images 100, tables 50.
selection is always null in Node/fromHTML contexts.

## Metadata sources (first match wins)

title:       og:title → twitter:title → first <h1> → document.title (strips " | Site Name" suffixes)
byline:      author → article:author → byl → parsely-author
hero:        og:image → twitter:image → twitter:image:src
canonicalUrl: <link rel="canonical"> → url
siteName:    og:site_name
language:    <html lang> → meta[name=language]
publishedAt: article:published_time → date
modifiedAt:  article:modified_time → lastmod

Images fall back to data-src when src is absent (lazy-load pattern).

## Docs

- README: https://github.com/yrstm/mantis#readme
- Source: https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/mantis.js
- Types: https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/mantis.d.ts
- Examples: https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/tree/main/examples

## Package

CommonJS entry: mantis.js | Runtime dependencies: none
